description: A 'herani' is a direction or pattern found across ordered observations. Rising river readings over several visits may form a trend. The word reports the selected series and period. Cause needs evidence of its own, and the next observation remains open.
articulatory_notes: Open the glottis for 'h' and move into true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip and open into stressed central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Breathy 'he' recalls the opening of 'helui' (change), then the tongue takes a direction through stressed 'ra' and settles at the fine dental point of 'ni'. The motion ends at the latest mark rather than predicting another.
usage_notes: The noun is countable. Its series and period define the pattern; scale, method, and uncertainty belong with the record. A trend belongs to one ordered series. 'hosari' compares variables, while causal explanation and future inference each add a claim.

examples.phi: ha herani ha nomiki thuroa shelo.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A trend names patterned change without turning continuity into certainty or one selected time window into an essence.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A trend states its scope and uncertainty, so an apparent direction cannot masquerade as unquestionable authority.

semantic_domains.cognition: a pattern inferred from an ordered series of observations
semantic_domains.temporal: direction or recurrence assessed across time or ordered states
